meta_title: For Students: design with AI to Solve can't debug code (Advanced Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to design with AI and overcome can't debug code. A Advanced-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Design with AI: Debugging Strategies for Advanced Students
content.seo_description: Master AI-driven debugging for your portfolio projects. Learn to use Antigravity and Claude Code to resolve complex errors and accelerate your development speed.
content.answer_block: Stop fighting syntax errors and start architecting solutions. By shifting from reactive debugging to proactive design with AI, you treat code as a modular system. Use Antigravity to map logical flows and Claude Code to execute structural refactors, turning your most frustrating bugs into clear, actionable design improvements.
content.intro:
The screen glows with a sea of red error logs—a familiar, suffocating silence that follows a broken build. You’ve spent hours chasing a race condition or a dependency mismatch, feeling the weight of the ‘junior’ label pressing down on your potential. It’s the wall every developer hits, but the truly elite don't just debug; they redesign the problem away.

Today, we pivot. You are moving from a developer who fixes lines of code to an architect who designs with AI. By leveraging Antigravity for structural logic and Claude Code for autonomous execution, you will transform your debugging workflow from a frantic search into a controlled engineering exercise. Welcome to the next phase of your career.

steps.title: Map the Logic Flow
steps.description: Before touching the code, visualize the system state. Use AI to map the dependency graph to find exactly where the state corruption occurs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current file structure and identify the data flow bottlenecks causing this runtime error. Create a dependency map.

steps.title: Architectural Refactoring
steps.description: Don't patch the bug; refactor the design pattern to prevent the class of error from recurring.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Propose a design pattern shift (e.g., state machine or observer) to eliminate the race condition identified in the logs.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Refactor this module to use a cleaner state management pattern'

steps.title: Autonomous Unit Generation
steps.description: Use Claude Code to write robust regression tests that verify the fix and prevent future regressions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a suite of unit tests that target the specific edge cases causing the current build failure.

steps.title: Iterative Implementation
steps.description: Execute the refactor in small, verifiable chunks to ensure the system remains stable throughout the process.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a step-by-step implementation plan for the refactor, prioritizing low-risk changes first.

steps.title: Final System Validation
steps.description: Perform a final integration check to ensure the architectural changes have resolved the root cause.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the final implementation against the initial error logs to confirm the root cause is eliminated.

pro_tips: Always treat your error logs as data points, not failures. Use AI to cluster them to find the 'parent' error.
Never accept the first AI suggestion; ask it for three distinct architectural approaches before choosing one.
Implement 'Design by Contract' patterns in your code to make debugging exponentially easier for AI agents.
Use Claude Code's memory feature to store your project's specific coding style guide for consistent outputs.
When debugging, ask the AI to explain the 'why' behind the bug, not just the 'how' of the fix, to accelerate your learning.
content.next_steps: Apply these debugging techniques to your current portfolio project, then document your refactoring process in a blog post to showcase your architectural thinking to potential employers.
content.conversion_block: You now possess the toolkit to solve any bug by redesigning the system from the ground up. Stop debugging and start building the future. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
